title: The chapbook tradition and the modern zine
sources: Cyril Davenport, The Book — Its History and Development (1907) — chapbook and pamphlet forms
Early English chapbook collections in the British Library and Internet Archive (pre-1928 examples)
notes: Historical reading article: chapbooks as the early-modern equivalent of zines — cheap, often single-sheet or few-page, printed on one side and folded, sold at fairs and markets. The lineage from chapbooks through pamphlets to the 1970s/80s photocopier zine. What the zine inherits: the fold-and-staple structure, the low-cost production ethic, the self-published voice.
